description: Close remaining mobile responsiveness gaps in the docs site without breaking any existing desktop experience. Three focused changes: CSS-only tab scroll affordance, explicit viewport meta, and a visual QA pass to catch any remaining issues.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Add explicit viewport meta dimensions
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the viewport to be explicitly configured so that the site renders predictably on all mobile devices without relying on framework defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: The viewport export in docs/app/layout.tsx includes width: 'device-width' and initialScale: 1 alongside the existing viewportFit: 'cover'
No other properties in the viewport export are changed or removed
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/app/layout.tsx. The viewport export is at the bottom of the file (around line 35-37). Currently it only has `viewportFit: 'cover'`. Add `width: 'device-width'` and `initialScale: 1` to the same object. The Viewport type from 'next' already supports these fields. This is a one-line change — just add two properties to the existing object literal.

userStories.title: CSS-only tab scroll fade indicator
userStories.description: As a mobile user viewing tabbed content, I want a visual cue that more tabs exist offscreen so that I know to scroll horizontally to find them.
acceptanceCriteria: The .retro-tabs-list element in docs/app/global.css has a mask-image CSS property that creates a horizontal fade effect on overflowing edges
The mask uses a linear-gradient that is fully opaque in the center and fades to transparent at the horizontal edges (left and right)

The fade only appears when content overflows — on non-overflowing tab lists the mask has no visible effect (the gradient transparent edges are outside the visible content area, so no content is clipped)
No JavaScript state, ResizeObserver, scroll event listeners, or additional DOM elements are added
No changes to docs/components/retro-tabs.tsx are needed — this is CSS-only
The existing desktop tab appearance is unchanged (tabs that fit without overflow look identical to before)
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/app/global.css. Find the existing .retro-tabs-list rules (around line 410-416) that hide the scrollbar. Add a mask-image property to the same selector. The approach: use `mask-image: linear-gradient(to right, transparent, black 2rem, black calc(100% - 2rem), transparent)` scoped to mobile only via a `@media (max-width: 639px)` block. This creates ~2rem fade zones at the left and right edges. On desktop where tabs typically fit, no mask is needed. Do NOT touch docs/components/retro-tabs.tsx. Do NOT add any JavaScript. The existing overflow-x-auto and scrollbar-width:none rules stay as-is — just layer the mask on top. Use standard CSS mask-image with -webkit-mask-image fallback for Safari.

userStories.title: Mobile visual QA pass — fix any remaining issues
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want all pages to render correctly on a 393px-wide viewport (iPhone 14 Pro) with no horizontal overflow, readable text, and tappable interactive elements.
acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal overflow on any page when viewed at 393px viewport width — verify by searching all component files and mdx-components.tsx for any fixed-width values, min-width utilities, or layout patterns that could cause overflow on narrow screens
All body text uses at least text-sm (14px) — no text-xs on body content (small labels/badges are acceptable)
Code blocks have overflow-auto and do not push the page width
Tables have an overflow wrapper and do not push the page width
The homepage hero section text does not overflow at 393px (the h1 with text-3xl sm:text-5xl md:text-7xl is already responsive — verify it still fits)
Feature cards on the homepage stack to single column on mobile (grid-cols-1 already set — verify)
How it works cards stack to single column on mobile (md:grid-cols-3 already set — verify)
All changes use responsive Tailwind classes (sm:, md:, lg:) to scope mobile-only modifications — no desktop styles are altered
No rounded classes are removed from any element
No theme variable values are changed
No min-h-11 or min-w-11 added to buttons inside constrained containers
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
This is a QA audit story. The agent should read through all component files in docs/components/ and docs/mdx-components.tsx looking for potential mobile issues at 393px width. Key files to check:

1. docs/components/global-header.tsx — verify the header works at 393px, check for overflow
2. docs/components/retroui-codeblock.tsx — verify code blocks have overflow-auto
3. docs/components/retro-steps.tsx — verify the step indicators position correctly on mobile (the CSS in global.css already has @media responsive positioning)
4. docs/components/retro-accordion.tsx — verify accordion content doesn't overflow
5. docs/components/retro-files.tsx — verify file tree doesn't overflow
6. docs/components/retro-page-footer.tsx — verify footer stacks on mobile
7. docs/components/retro-page-breadcrumb.tsx — verify breadcrumb wraps
8. docs/app/page.tsx — verify homepage sections at 393px
9. docs/mdx-components.tsx — verify table wrapper, code blocks, callouts

The user's context says most things already work. Focus on finding edge cases. If everything looks correct after review, document what was verified. If issues are found, fix them using responsive Tailwind classes. Read docs/CLAUDE.md before making any changes — the theme rules are strict. Do NOT change desktop styles. Do NOT add JavaScript for visual affordances.
